channel.title: BeastsNetwork | Privacy & Cybersecurity Blog
channel.description: Verified privacy and cybersecurity news from BeastsNetwork. Stay updated with accurate, sourced information on data protection, encryption, and digital security.

item.title: AI-Powered Phishing Attacks Surge 1,380% as Criminal Platforms Render MFA Obsolete
item.description: New research from Huntress reveals a massive 1,380% increase in device code phishing attacks in early 2026. Attackers exploit legitimate OAuth flows to bypass MFA, tricking users into authenticating on genuine Microsoft pages while attackers capture access tokens.
item.dc:creator: IT Security Guru

item.title: 24 Billion Records Exposed in Colossal Data Leak
item.description: Hackers claim to be selling 340 million OnlyFans user records, compiled from internal databases and previous breaches. The massive dataset includes emails, usernames, and account activity metrics, potentially exposing real identities of creators and subscribers.
item.dc:creator: Cybernews

item.title: CISA Urges Hardening of Fortinet Devices After Global Credential Exposure
item.description: CISA warns that malicious actors have targeted internet-accessible Fortinet devices across government and private sector organizations. The FortiBleed campaign involves exposure of credentials from approximately 74,000 Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways.
item.dc:creator: CISA

item.title: One Medical Healthcare Breach: 8.8 TB of Patient Data at Risk
item.description: ShinyHunters data extortion group claims to have stolen 8.8 TB of data from One Medical. Unauthorized access was identified on June 13, with access occurring between June 8-11. The group threatens to leak the data unless ransom demands are met by June 22.
item.dc:creator: HIPAA Journal

item.title: FortiBleed: 86,644 Fortinet Firewalls Compromised Globally
item.description: A Russian-speaking cybercriminal syndicate harvested verified administrator and VPN credentials from 86,644 Fortinet FortiGate firewalls across 194 countries. The incident surfaced publicly on June 13 when a security researcher discovered an exposed threat actor server.
item.dc:creator: Shattered.io

item.title: Consumer Data Privacy and Security Act of 2026
item.description: Senator Moran introduced S. 4211, the Consumer Data Privacy and Security Act of 2026. The bill aims to protect consumer privacy at the federal level. It was introduced on March 25 and last updated on June 15, 2026.
item.dc:creator: GovTrack.us

item.title: Signal Usernames Roll Out: Phone Number Privacy
item.description: Signal has begun rolling out usernames, allowing users to create unique handles for connections without sharing phone numbers. By default, phone numbers are now hidden from new contacts. These features are currently in public beta and rolling out to all users in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Freedom of the Press Foundation
